front: What is the main thread in a Java application?
back: The main thread is the initial thread that begins execution when a Java program starts. It is the thread from which all other 'child' threads are spawned. It is created automatically by the JVM.

front: How can you obtain a reference to the main thread object?
back: You can obtain a reference by calling `Thread.currentThread()` from within the main method. This method returns a reference to the currently executing thread object, which in this context is the main thread.

front: What is the default name and priority of the main thread?
back: The main thread's default name is 'main' and its default priority is `Thread.NORM_PRIORITY`, which is typically represented by the integer value 5.

front: What is the significance of the main thread in relation to program termination?
back: A Java program continues to execute until all non-daemon threads have finished. Since the main thread is non-daemon, the program will not terminate until the main thread exits, even if other threads are still running.

front: What happens if an uncaught exception occurs in the main thread?
back: If an exception is thrown in the main thread and not caught, the thread will terminate. However, if other user threads are still active, the program will continue running until all user threads finish executing.
